text:
I had an old fisherman friend who loved to fish all their lives. One autumn, he couldnned nothing but that old boat. I felt sorry for him.

He told me an old tale about a fisherman who went out one last time, angling into early fall.

He brought home a fish so big it took him months to fillet it.

That's him.

He fished that big yellow catfish.

There's a small island nearby where the fishermen liked to fish. And one fateful day…

He didn't come home.

And he never fished again.

They say he went out fishing just before the storm. They think a great catfish grabbed him. They found him that way next to that one big hook.

That's him.

And they say he won't talk much anymore.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
